markdown:
Based on the headline model success percentages reported in each paper's main results:

**Easiest → Hardest:**

1. **GAIA (easiest)** — GPT-4 equipped with plugins: **15%** overall success rate [1]. On the easiest Level 1 subset, the best model reaches ~30% [2].

2. **WebArena (middle)** — Best GPT-4-based agent: **14.41%** end-to-end task success rate [3].

3. **SWE-bench (hardest)** — Best performing model (Claude 2) with BM25 retrieval: **1.96%** resolve rate [4] [5].

So the gap between GAIA/WebArena and SWE-bench is roughly an order of magnitude: GAIA and WebArena are comparably difficult for frontier models (≈14–15%), while SWE-bench is dramatically harder (under 2% for the best model in the standard BM25 retrieval setting).

Sources
[1] GAIA: a benchmark for General AI Assistants, unsectioned, p. 1
[2] GAIA: a benchmark for General AI Assistants, unsectioned, p. 21
[3] WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents, unsectioned, p. 1
[4] SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?, unsectioned, p. 2
[5] SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?, unsectioned, p. 6
